name: mfa-bypass
description: Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) kit harvests both password and session cookie. MFA looks satisfied to Entra but the session is the attacker's. Trigger: Detect-AnomalousLocationSignIn + Detect-SuspiciousInboxRule (the AiTM kit always plants the same rule).
trigger: Sign-in from never-before-seen country with MFA satisfied + the classic AiTM rule signature appeared within minutes.
expectedFindings: MFA-completed sign-in from anomalous geo (the smoking gun)
Inbox rule that filters financial keywords + forwards externally
Recent OAuth consent grant to a third-party app (common AiTM persistence)
recoveryNotes: Revoke all OAuth consents for the affected user via Entra portal. Block the source IP at the perimeter. Search the tenant for the same inbox-rule signature on other accounts (the kit often hits a list).
